    15. Day 4: The Contrarian Question
</h2>

<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p>Dear Trainer,</p><p>After the last 3 days of analysis, your friend will now feel seen and heard. They will know that you're in the best position to help them because you know things about them now that few people really care to know.</p><p>Now we can get to the nitty gritty.</p><p>No more fooling around because you don't have to. </p><p>You have their trust now.</p><p>Ask these 3 questions over and over again until you get solid answers.</p><blockquote><strong>1. What do you bring to the world that no-one else does?</strong></blockquote><blockquote><strong>2. Why should anyone care about what you're doing?</strong></blockquote><blockquote><strong>3. What would you like to change about how the world is right now?</strong></blockquote><blockquote><strong>4. Where would you like to see the world evolve to? Can you see yourself as part of a movement taking us there?</strong></blockquote><p>Tell the student: "You have no message until you stand for something that can create enemies."</p><p>You cannot move past these questions. You need to have strong compelling answers to these questions. Even if it takes days to figure out.</p><p>If you've probed deeply, the student should have at least the embryo of a message by now.</p><p>Have the student write out that message. As a full sentence. It should express their vision of a future world and how they plan on helping the world get there.</p><p>*And look, I know this looks like a short paragraph... But if you're doing it right, this can take hours. But it's worth it. You may be responsible for your friend's most important artistic breakthrough today. </p><p>Keep going.</p><p>Ok. Time to 10X the message now.</p><p>You will help your friend figure out how to execute their message so that it touches the deepest nerve with their audience.</p><p>This is why you ask "The Contrarian Question" (created by Peter Theil).</p><h2><strong>"What do you believe to be true, that the majority of society (or culture) does NOT believe to be true right now?"</strong></h2><p>“Most people believe in x, but you think that the truth is the opposite of x.”</p><p>A contrarian belief is one that is not currently held by the masses to be true, but YOU believe it should be a common truth. You think it’s the wave of the future. </p><p>The student will want to give you answers of things that everyone already believes in. Like equal rights, the environment,"love is the answer"... You know... Generic things. Things the majority already agree on. Remind them that you're looking for something that THEY believe in that MOST PEOPLE DON'T BELIEVE. A topic where they stand alone or with a very small group. So much so that they don't bring it up often.</p><p>Give the student examples.</p><p>Here are a few answers to the contrarian beliefs that are held by a tiny portion of society. Any of these may become reality one day:</p><p>“Cryptocurrency will replace common currency in the future.”</p><p>“Women replacing men in powerful positions will give us a better world.”</p><p>“Naturopathy is the best way to treat all human illnesses, including cancer.”</p><p>But contrarian views DO become the norm eventually.</p><p>People used to think that singing in Spanish was a death sentence if you wanted to make pop music in America. But a few people believed the opposite... A contrarian view that is slowly winning that war on the pop charts now. </p><p>It takes courage to hold a view that goes against the majority.</p><p>Ask the student: What view do they hold that goes against "group think"?</p><h1><strong>THEY CAN'T COME UP WITH A CONTRARIAN QUESTION?</strong></h1><p>If they can't come up with a contrarian statement, they’ll have trouble finding their message.</p><p>PUSH THEM A BIT: "So basically you're saying that 1. You agree with the majority of people about everything? Really? 2. You have no original thought about anything? 3. You're ok with the world (and art) just the way it is? Is that really true?"</p><p>Give them more examples:</p><p>When Kim Kardashian came on the scene, most people believed that being super-skinny and having thin eyebrows was the ideal in sexiness for a women. But her whole family started pushing a new feminine ideal: thick eye-brows and big asses. That was contrarian THEN... It was a statement that women found refreshing and SLOWLY started adopting.</p><p>But you can't use that one anymore. It was contrarian THEN... But it isn't anymore. </p><p>Ask them again "What ideas do they hold dear that haven't been adopted by the masses yet?"</p><p><strong>The Mystery School contrarian ideas:</strong></p><p>You're were attracted to The Mystery School because of it's contrarian views. In a world still thriving on content, Father Bronques dared to say:</p><p>"Let's start making art instead of making content."</p><p>"The artist should treat the creative process like they're an athlete training."</p><p>"Quality AND quantity is attainable to everyone."</p><p>"The key to mental health (for artists) is to lead an UNBALANCED life. A life of making MORE art."</p><p>"A creative without a message is a craftsperson, not an artist."</p><p>"We are leaving the "era of image" and entering the "age of message".</p><p>"Social media isn't bad. It's just the way you use it that's bad."</p><p>"Don't make your art for everybody. Make it for the precious few. That's the only chance you have of everybody loving it."</p><p>Etc.</p><p>Ask them again... "What is YOUR contrarian statement? What do you believe to be true, that the majority of people don't believe to be true... At least not yet?" </p><h1><strong>ONCE THEY FIND THEIR CONTRARIAN STATEMENT</strong></h1><p>This will make clear a promised land that your friend is trying to bring people to. But it also exposes to them who their haters will be in the process! It IS contrarian after all, right? The masses will disagree.</p><p>Once they find something real... This is the moment when you ask your friend: "Are you ready to stand for this? Are you ready to stand for something that will make you loved by a some AND hated by others? Are you ready for that?"</p><p>Stop for the day.</p><p>Tomorrow you will be giving the student's message "The Kanye Test". Tell them that.</p><p>You need to let the subconscious mind move around a little bit before you go hard again.</p><p></p>
